input.prompt:

50	  gitStatus: "git.status",
51	  gitRunStackedAction: "git.runStackedAction",
52	  gitListBranches: "git.listBranches",
53	  gitCreateWorktree: "git.createWorktree",
54	  gitRemoveWorktree: "git.removeWorktree",

56	  gitCheckout: "git.checkout",
57	  gitInit: "git.init",

72	// ── Push Event Channels ──────────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	export const WS_CHANNELS = {
75	  terminalEvent: "terminal.event",
76	  serverWelcome: "server.welcome",
77	  serverConfigUpdated: "server.configUpdated",
78	} as const;

80	// -- Tagged Union of all request body schemas ─────────────────────────
81	
82	const tagRequestBody = <const Tag extends string, const Fields extends Schema.Struct.Fields>(
83	  tag: Tag,
84	  schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>,
85	) =>
86	  schema.mapFields(
87	    Struct.assign({ _tag: Schema.tag(tag) }),
88	    // PreserveChecks is safe here. No existing schema should have checks depending on the tag
89	    { unsafePreserveChecks: true },
90	  );
91	
92	const WebSocketRequestBody = Schema.Union([
93	  // Orchestration methods
94	  tagRequestBody(
95	    ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.dispatchCommand,
96	    Schema.Struct({ command: ClientOrchestrationCommand }),
97	  ),
98	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getSnapshot, OrchestrationGetSnapshotInput),
99	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getTurnDiff, OrchestrationGetTurnDiffInput),
100	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.getFullThreadDiff, OrchestrationGetFullThreadDiffInput),
101	  tagRequestBody(ORCHESTRATION_WS_METHODS.replayEvents, OrchestrationReplayEventsInput),
102	
103	  // Project Search
104	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsSearchEntries, ProjectSearchEntriesInput),
105	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.projectsWriteFile, ProjectWriteFileInput),
106	
107	  // Shell methods
108	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.shellOpenInEditor, OpenInEditorInput),
109	
110	  // Git methods
111	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitPull, GitPullInput),
112	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitStatus, GitStatusInput),
113	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRunStackedAction, GitRunStackedActionInput),
114	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitListBranches, GitListBranchesInput),
115	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateWorktree, GitCreateWorktreeInput),
116	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitRemoveWorktree, GitRemoveWorktreeInput),
117	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCreateBranch, GitCreateBranchInput),
118	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitCheckout, GitCheckoutInput),
119	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.gitInit, GitInitInput),
120	
121	  // Terminal methods
122	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalOpen, TerminalOpenInput),
123	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalWrite, TerminalWriteInput),
124	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalResize, TerminalResizeInput),
125	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClear, TerminalClearInput),
126	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalRestart, TerminalRestartInput),
127	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.terminalClose, TerminalCloseInput),
128	
129	  // Server meta
130	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverGetConfig, Schema.Struct({})),
131	  tagRequestBody(WS_METHODS.serverUpsertKeybinding, KeybindingRule),
132	]);
133	
134	export const WebSocketRequest = Schema.Struct({
135	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
136	  body: WebSocketRequestBody,
137	});
138	export type WebSocketRequest = typeof WebSocketRequest.Type;
139	
140	export const WebSocketResponse = Schema.Struct({
141	  id: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
142	  result: Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown),
143	  error: Schema.optional(
144	    Schema.Struct({
145	      message: Schema.String,
146	    }),
147	  ),
148	});
149	export type WebSocketResponse = typeof WebSocketResponse.Type;
150	
151	export const WsPush = Schema.Struct({
152	  type: Schema.Literal("push"),
153	  channel: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
154	  data: Schema.Unknown,
155	});
156	export type WsPush = typeof WsPush.Type;
157	
158	// ── Union of all server → client messages ─────────────────────────────
159	
160	export const WsResponse = Schema.Union([WebSocketResponse, WsPush]);
161	export type WsResponse = typeof WsResponse.Type;
162	
163	// ── Server welcome payload ───────────────────────────────────────────
164	
165	export const WsWelcomePayload = Schema.Struct({
166	  cwd: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
167	  projectName: TrimmedNonEmptyString,
168	  bootstrapProjectId: Schema.optional(ProjectId),
169	  bootstrapThreadId: Schema.optional(ThreadId),
170	});
171	export type WsWelcomePayload = typeof WsWelcomePayload.Type;

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Exploration of `apps/web`

I've thoroughly explored the `apps/web` directory structure and analyzed the project configuration. Here's a detailed breakdown:

### 1. COMPLETE FILE STRUCTURE

**Top-level source files in `apps/web/src/`:**
- `main.tsx` - Entry point
- `router.ts` - Router configuration
- `store.ts` - Main Zustand store
- `index.css` - Global styles with Tailwind and CSS variables
- `appSettings.ts` - Application settings store with localStorage
- `composerDraftStore.ts` - Composer draft state (Zustand + persist middleware)
- `terminalStateStore.ts` - Terminal UI state (Zustand + persist middleware)
- `types.ts` - TypeScript type definitions
- `env.ts`, `nativeApi.ts`, `branding.ts` - Configuration files

**Subdirectories:**
- `components/` (25 files) - React components
  - `ui/` (30+ base UI components using @base-ui/react)
  - Major components: `ChatView.tsx`, `DiffPanel.tsx`, `GitActionsControl.tsx`, `Sidebar.tsx`, `DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`, etc.
- `routes/` (5 files) - TanStack Router pages
  - `__root.tsx` - Root layout with provider composition
  - `_chat.tsx`, `_chat.$threadId.tsx`, `_chat.settings.tsx`
- `hooks/` (3 files) - Custom hooks
  - `useTheme.ts` - Theme management
  - `useMediaQuery.ts`, `useTurnDiffSummaries.ts`
- `lib/` (20+ files) - Utilities and helpers
  - `utils.ts` - Common utilities (cn, ID generators)
  - React Query hooks for server queries
  - Diff rendering, terminal cleanup logic

**Total:** 139 TypeScript/TSX files

---

### 2. CSS/STYLING SETUP

**Primary Styling Stack:**
- **Tailwind CSS v4** with `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin
- **CSS Variables** (not PostCSS, but CSS custom properties in `:root`)
- **Semantic Color System** with light/dark modes

**CSS Variables Structure** (`index.css` lines 64-121):
```
Light mode (:root):
  --background, --foreground, --card, --popover
  --primary, --primary-foreground
  --secondary, --secondary-foreground
  --accent, --accent-foreground
  --destructive, --destructive-foreground
  --muted, --muted-foreground
  --border, --input
  --ring
  --info, --success, --warning (status colors)
  --radius (base), --radius-sm/md/lg/xl/2xl/3xl/4xl

Dark mode (@variant dark):
  Same structure with adjusted color-mix values
```

**Key CSS Features:**
- Custom dark variant with `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))`
- Theme-aware colors using `oklch()` color space for primary/ring
- Global resets in `@layer base`
- Tailwind `@theme` inline for custom animations and aliases
- Special styling for:
  - Terminal scrollbars (xterm compatibility)
  - Chat markdown rendering
  - Diff panel theming
  - Drag region (frameless titlebar support for Electron)
  - No-transitions class for smooth theme switching

**Tailwind Configuration:**
- Imported via Vite plugin in `vite.config.ts`
- No separate `tailwind.config.js` (uses defaults + CSS custom properties)
- Class variance authority (`cva`) for component variants

---

### 3. REACT CONTEXT/PROVIDERS STRUCTURE

**Provider Composition Hierarchy** (in `router.ts` and `__root.tsx`):

1. **Root Level** (in `router.ts` - `getRouter()` function):
   ```
   QueryClientProvider (TanStack React Query)
     └─ StoreProvider (from store.ts)
   ```

2. **Root Route** (in `routes/__root.tsx` - `RootRouteView`):
   ```
   ToastProvider
     └─ AnchoredToastProvider
         └─ EventRouter (internal component for domain events)
         └─ DesktopProjectBootstrap
         └─ Outlet (route children)
   ```

3. **Additional Providers Used:**
   - `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` - For diff rendering with worker pools (uses `@pierre/diffs/react`)
   - `WorkerPoolContextProvider` - From diff library

**Custom Hooks:**
- `useTheme()` - Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }`
- `useAppSettings()` - Returns `{ settings, updateSettings, resetSettings, defaults }`
- `useStore()` - Zustand store hook for app state
- `useMediaQuery()` - Media query matching

---

### 4. LOCAL STORAGE USAGE

**Five distinct localStorage implementations:**

| Key | File | Purpose | Type |
|-----|------|---------|------|
| `"t3code:theme"` | `useTheme.ts` | Theme preference (light/dark/system) | useSyncExternalStore |
| `"t3code:app-settings:v1"` | `appSettings.ts` | App settings (models, service tier, codex paths) | useSyncExternalStore |
| `"t3code:renderer-state:v8"` | `store.ts` | Project expanded state | Zustand + persist |
| `"t3code:composer-drafts:v1"` | `composerDraftStore.ts` | Composer draft messages/attachments | Zustand + persist |
| `"t3code:terminal-state:v1"` | `terminalStateStore.ts` | Terminal UI state per thread | Zustand + persist |

**Storage Pattern:**
- Uses `localStorage` directly in most cases
- Zustand stores use `createJSONStorage` middleware with localStorage
- Cross-tab sync via `storage` event listener
- Graceful fallbacks for SSR (checks `typeof window`)
- Legacy key cleanup (v1-v7 for renderer state)

---

### 5. COMPONENT ORGANIZATION & UI PATTERNS

**Base UI Component Library:**
- Built on `@base-ui/react` primitives
- All in `components/ui/` directory
- Pattern: Export component + primitive wrapper
- Variants using `cva` (class-variance-authority)

**Base UI Components** (30+ components):
```
Forms:
  - input.tsx, textarea.tsx, label.tsx, field.tsx, fieldset.tsx, form.tsx
  - checkbox.tsx, radio-group.tsx, switch.tsx, select.tsx
  - combobox.tsx, autocomplete.tsx

Feedback:
  - alert.tsx, alert-dialog.tsx, toast.tsx (with logic.ts)
  - skeleton.tsx, spinner.tsx, empty.tsx

Navigation:
  - button.tsx, menu.tsx, toggle.tsx, toggle-group.tsx
  - sidebar.tsx, sheet.tsx (drawer)

Layout:
  - card.tsx, group.tsx, input-group.tsx, fieldset.tsx
  - scroll-area.tsx, separator.tsx

Utility:
  - badge.tsx, kbd.tsx, popover.tsx, tooltip.tsx
  - collapsible.tsx, dialog.tsx, command.tsx
```

**Major App Components:**
- `ChatView.tsx` - Main chat interface
- `DiffPanel.tsx` - Diff viewer
- `Sidebar.tsx` - Left navigation
- `BranchToolbar.tsx` - Git branch controls
- `GitActionsControl.tsx` - Git operations
- `ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx` - Terminal display
- `ComposerPromptEditor.tsx` - Message input
- `ProjectScriptsControl.tsx` - Script runner

**Pattern Observations:**
- Components use semantic HTML from `@base-ui/react`
- Heavy use of Tailwind classes with `cn()` utility
- Props typed with TypeScript
- Separate `.logic.ts` files for business logic (tested separately)
- Browser-specific variants: `ChatView.browser.tsx` vs `ChatView.tsx`

---

### 6. ENTRY POINT & PROVIDER COMPOSITION

**`main.tsx` Flow:**
```typescript
1. Import styles (@xterm/xterm/css/xterm.css, ./index.css)
2. Create router via getRouter(history) → sets up QueryClientProvider + StoreProvider
3. Render RouterProvider with router instance
4. In __root.tsx: ToastProvider + AnchoredToastProvider wrap route outlet
5. EventRouter component listens to server domain events and syncs state
6. DiffWorkerPoolProvider injected when needed (loaded by chat components)
```

**Provider Wrap Chain:**
```
React.StrictMode (in main.tsx)
  ├─ RouterProvider
  │   └─ QueryClientProvider
  │       └─ StoreProvider (Fragment with useEffect for persistence)
  │           └─ TanStack Router (Outlet)
  │               └─ ToastProvider (root route)
  │                   └─ AnchoredToastProvider
  │                       └─ EventRouter (event listener)
  │                           └─ DiffWorkerPoolProvider (conditional)
  │                               └─ Route Outlet (page content)
```

---

### 7. EXISTING THEME-RELATED CODE

**Theme Implementation** (`hooks/useTheme.ts`):
- **Storage Key:** `"t3code:theme"`
- **Values:** `"light"` | `"dark"` | `"system"`
- **Default:** `"system"` (respects OS preference)
- **Sync Method:** `useSyncExternalStore` pattern (external store)

**Theme Application:**
- DOM manipulation: toggles `"dark"` class on `<html>` element
- Supports smooth transitions with `no-transitions` class
- Listens to system preference changes via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
- Cross-tab sync via `storage` event

**Diff Theme Bridge:**
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` calls `workerPool.setRenderOptions()` when theme changes
- `resolveDiffThemeName()` maps resolved theme to diff library theme names

**Global Theme Colors:**
- CSS variables in `:root` and `@variant dark` (lines 64-121 of `index.css`)
- Tailwind automatically picks up CSS variables via `var(--colorname)` references
- Primary color: `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` (light) / `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` (dark)

---

### 8. KEY CONFIGURATION FILES

**`package.json`:**
- React 19, Vite 8 beta, TypeScript
- TanStack Router, React Query, Zustand
- @base-ui/react, Tailwind CSS 4
- Babel React compiler for optimization

**`tsconfig.json`:**
- Path alias: `"~/*": ["./src/*"]`
- JSX: `"react-jsx"`
- Effect language service plugin for type safety

**`vite.config.ts`:**
- Plugins: TanStack Router, React with compiler, Tailwind
- Port: 5733 (configurable)
- HMR: WebSocket protocol for Electron compatibility
- Sourcemap: Configurable via `T3CODE_WEB_SOURCEMAP` env var

**`components.json`:**
- Shadcn-style component metadata

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Total Files** | 139 (TSX/TS) |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS 4 + CSS variables |
| **State Management** | Zustand (store, composerDraftStore, terminalStateStore) |
| **Server State** | TanStack React Query |
| **Router** | TanStack React Router v1 |
| **UI Library** | @base-ui/react primitives + custom wrapper components |
| **Theme System** | Custom hook (useSyncExternalStore) + localStorage + CSS class toggle |
| **localStorage Keys** | 5 (theme, app-settings, renderer-state, composer-drafts, terminal-state) |
| **Provider Count** | 7 (QueryClient, Store, Toast, AnchoredToast, EventRouter, DiffWorkerPool, RouterProvider) |

This is a modern, well-structured React + TypeScript application with comprehensive state management, theme support, and semantic UI components. All styling is configuration-driven through CSS variables with Tailwind's Vite integration.

- **Values:** `"light"` | `"dark"` | `"system"`
- **Default:** `"system"` (respects OS preference)
- **Sync Method:** `useSyncExternalStore` pattern (external store)

**Theme Application:**
- DOM manipulation: toggles `"dark"` class on `<html>` element
- Supports smooth transitions with `no-transitions` class
- Listens to system preference changes via `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")`
- Cross-tab sync via `storage` event

**Diff Theme Bridge:**
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` calls `workerPool.setRenderOptions()` when theme changes
- `resolveDiffThemeName()` maps resolved theme to diff library theme names

**Global Theme Colors:**
- CSS variables in `:root` and `@variant dark` (lines 64-121 of `index.css`)
- Tailwind automatically picks up CSS variables via `var(--colorname)` references
- Primary color: `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` (light) / `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` (dark)

---

### 8. KEY CONFIGURATION FILES

**`package.json`:**
- React 19, Vite 8 beta, TypeScript
- TanStack Router, React Query, Zustand
- @base-ui/react, Tailwind CSS 4
- Babel React compiler for optimization

**`tsconfig.json`:**
- Path alias: `"~/*": ["./src/*"]`
- JSX: `"react-jsx"`
- Effect language service plugin for type safety

**`vite.config.ts`:**
- Plugins: TanStack Router, React with compiler, Tailwind
- Port: 5733 (configurable)
- HMR: WebSocket protocol for Electron compatibility
- Sourcemap: Configurable via `T3CODE_WEB_SOURCEMAP` env var

**`components.json`:**
- Shadcn-style component metadata

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Total Files** | 139 (TSX/TS) |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS 4 + CSS variables |
| **State Management** | Zustand (store, composerDraftStore, terminalStateStore) |
| **Server State** | TanStack React Query |
| **Router** | TanStack React Router v1 |
| **UI Library** | @base-ui/react primitives + custom wrapper components |
| **Theme System** | Custom hook (useSyncExternalStore) + localStorage + CSS class toggle |
| **localStorage Keys** | 5 (theme, app-settings, renderer-state, composer-drafts, terminal-state) |
| **Provider Count** | 7 (QueryClient, Store, Toast, AnchoredToast, EventRouter, DiffWorkerPool, RouterProvider) |

This is a modern, well-structured React + TypeScript application with comprehensive state management, theme support, and semantic UI components. All styling is configuration-driven through CSS variables with Tailwind's Vite integration.

### 3. TYPESCRIPT TYPES AND SCHEMAS

- `ApprovalRequestId`, `CheckpointRef`

#### Major Type Categories

**Model Management** (model.ts)

- `ModelSlug` union of built-in models + string extensibility
- Model option lookups: `MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER`, `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER`
- Reasoning effort options by provider

**Editor Configuration** (editor.ts)
- Supported editors: Cursor, VS Code, Zed, File Manager
- `EditorId` and `OpenInEditorInput` schemas

**Terminal I/O** (terminal.ts)
- Input schemas: `TerminalOpenInput`, `TerminalWriteInput`, `TerminalResizeInput`, `TerminalCloseInput`
- Output: `TerminalSessionSnapshot` with status (starting/running/exited/error)
- Events: Union type `TerminalEvent` with variants for started/output/exited/error/cleared/restarted/activity

**Provider Session** (provider.ts)
- `ProviderSession` - session state tracking
- `ProviderSessionStartInput` - initialization parameters
- `ProviderSendTurnInput` - message with attachments
- `ProviderEvent` - structured provider events

**Orchestration** (orchestration.ts) - **MOST COMPREHENSIVE**
- `OrchestrationReadModel` - complete snapshot of projects, threads, activities
- `OrchestrationThread` - full thread state with messages, plans, activities, checkpoints
- `OrchestrationProject` - project metadata and scripts
- `OrchestrationMessage` - conversation messages with role/attachments
- `ChatAttachment` - image attachments with size validation
- `ProjectScript` - automation scripts with icons
- Command types: `ProjectCreateCommand`, `ProjectMetaUpdateCommand`, etc.
- Provider constants: approval policies, sandbox modes, service tiers, interaction modes

**WebSocket Protocol** (ws.ts)
- `WebSocketRequest` - tagged union of all RPC methods
- `WebSocketResponse` - result or error response
- `WsPush` - server-to-client push events
- Defines `WS_METHODS` constants for all callable methods

**Git Operations** (git.ts)
- Input schemas: `GitStatusInput`, `GitPullInput`, `GitRunStackedActionInput`
- `GitBranch`, `GitWorktree` structures
- Result schemas with detailed branch, commit, push, and PR status
- Supports stacked actions: commit, commit_push, commit_push_pr

**Server Configuration** (server.ts)
- `ServerConfig` - current server state and settings
- `ServerProviderStatus` - per-provider authentication and availability
- `ServerConfigIssue` - configuration validation issues
- `KeybindingsConfig` - resolved keybindings

**Keybindings** (keybindings.ts)
- `KeybindingCommand` - static commands + script.*.run pattern
- `KeybindingRule` - key/command/when expression
- `KeybindingWhenNode` - recursive when-expression AST (identifier/not/and/or)
- `ResolvedKeybindingRule` - compiled shortcut with parsed AST

**Project Operations** (project.ts)
- `ProjectSearchEntriesInput` - file search with limit
- `ProjectEntry` - file/directory with metadata
- `ProjectWriteFileInput` - create/update file with content

**Provider Runtime** (providerRuntime.ts)
- `RuntimeEventRaw` - normalized provider event wrapper
- Extensive type enums for runtime states:
  - Session states: starting, ready, running, waiting, stopped, error
  - Thread states: active, idle, archived, closed, compacted, error
  - Turn states: completed, failed, interrupted, cancelled
  - Item types: 16 distinct types (user_message, command_execution, etc.)
  - Request types: 9 approval/input request types
  - Event types: 50+ runtime event type discriminants

- `src/persistence/` - persistence layer for all domain entities

### 5. UTILITY PATTERNS AND TYPE PATTERNS

#### Shared Utility Patterns

**Git utilities** (packages/shared/src/git.ts):
```typescript
sanitizeBranchFragment(raw: string): string
sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw: string): string
resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(existingBranchNames, preferredBranch): string
```

**Model resolution** (packages/shared/src/model.ts):
```typescript
getModelOptions(provider): ModelOption[]
getDefaultModel(provider): ModelSlug
normalizeModelSlug(model, provider): ModelSlug | null
resolveModelSlug(model, provider): ModelSlug
getReasoningEffortOptions(provider): CodexReasoningEffort[]
```

**Port availability** (packages/shared/src/Net.ts):
- Effect-based `NetService` with methods:
  - `canListenOnHost(port, host)`
  - `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port)`
  - `reserveLoopbackPort(host)`
  - `findAvailablePort(preferred)`

**Shell utilities** (packages/shared/src/shell.ts):
```typescript
extractPathFromShellOutput(output): string | null
readPathFromLoginShell(shell, execFile): string | undefined
```

**Logging** (packages/shared/src/logging.ts):
- `RotatingFileSink` class with:
  - Automatic file rotation when max size reached
  - Backup pruning
  - Error handling with optional throw mode

#### Contract Type Patterns

**Effect Schema-based validation:**
- All contract types use `Schema` from Effect library
- Branded types via `Schema.brand()`
- Discriminated unions with `.pipe()` chains
- Max length/value checks: `.check(Schema.isMaxLength(n))`
- Range checks: `.check(Schema.isGreaterThanOrEqualTo(n))`
- Pattern validation: `.check(Schema.isPattern(/regex/))`

**Tagged Union pattern:**
```typescript
// Example from ws.ts - tagRequestBody helper
tagRequestBody<Tag, Fields>(tag: Tag, schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>)
  // Creates tagged union discriminated by _tag field
```

**Hierarchical Input Schemas:**
```typescript
// Terminal schemas compose field inheritance
const TerminalSessionInput = Schema.Struct({
  ...TerminalThreadInput.fields,  // Spread composition
  terminalId: TerminalIdWithDefaultSchema
});
```

**Recursive Type Definitions:**
```typescript
// KeybindingWhenNode - recursive when-expression AST
Schema.Union([
  Schema.Struct({ type: Literal("not"), node: Schema.suspend(...) }),
  Schema.Struct({ type: Literal("and"), left: Schema.suspend(...), right: Schema.suspend(...) }),
  // ...
])
```

**Default Value Application:**
```typescript
TerminalIdWithDefaultSchema.pipe(
  Schema.withDecodingDefault(() => DEFAULT_TERMINAL_ID)
)
```

### DEPENDENCY RELATIONSHIPS

```
apps/web
  ├── depends on: @t3tools/contracts (types for state, WebSocket)
  └── depends on: @t3tools/shared/model (model normalization)

apps/server
  ├── depends on: @t3tools/contracts (types for orchestration, persistence)
  ├── depends on: @t3tools/shared/Net (port discovery)
  └── depends on: @t3tools/shared/logging (file logging)

@t3tools/shared
  └── depends on: @t3tools/contracts (uses model types from contracts)

@t3tools/contracts
  └── depends on: effect (Schema library only)
```

### KEY OBSERVATIONS

1. **Clear separation of concerns**: `contracts` = types/schemas, `shared` = implementations
2. **Effect-based architecture**: Heavy use of Effect's Schema for validation and branded types
3. **No bidirectional dependencies**: contracts is foundational, shared imports from contracts
4. **Direct source consumption**: Both packages served as ESM source files to dev apps
5. **Branded IDs everywhere**: All entity IDs are branded strings for type safety
6. **Comprehensive protocol definitions**: WebSocket and IPC protocols fully typed via schemas
7. **65+ Schema types** across contracts covering all domain entities
8. **Minimal external dependencies**: Only `effect` as runtime dependency (and platform-specific bindings)

```typescript
extractPathFromShellOutput(output): string | null
readPathFromLoginShell(shell, execFile): string | undefined
```

**Logging** (packages/shared/src/logging.ts):
- `RotatingFileSink` class with:
  - Automatic file rotation when max size reached
  - Backup pruning
  - Error handling with optional throw mode

#### Contract Type Patterns

**Effect Schema-based validation:**
- All contract types use `Schema` from Effect library
- Branded types via `Schema.brand()`
- Discriminated unions with `.pipe()` chains
- Max length/value checks: `.check(Schema.isMaxLength(n))`
- Range checks: `.check(Schema.isGreaterThanOrEqualTo(n))`
- Pattern validation: `.check(Schema.isPattern(/regex/))`

**Tagged Union pattern:**
```typescript
// Example from ws.ts - tagRequestBody helper
tagRequestBody<Tag, Fields>(tag: Tag, schema: Schema.Struct<Fields>)
  // Creates tagged union discriminated by _tag field
```

**Hierarchical Input Schemas:**
```typescript
// Terminal schemas compose field inheritance
const TerminalSessionInput = Schema.Struct({
  ...TerminalThreadInput.fields,  // Spread composition
  terminalId: TerminalIdWithDefaultSchema
});
```

**Recursive Type Definitions:**
```typescript
// KeybindingWhenNode - recursive when-expression AST
Schema.Union([
  Schema.Struct({ type: Literal("not"), node: Schema.suspend(...) }),
  Schema.Struct({ type: Literal("and"), left: Schema.suspend(...), right: Schema.suspend(...) }),
  // ...
])
```

**Default Value Application:**
```typescript
TerminalIdWithDefaultSchema.pipe(
  Schema.withDecodingDefault(() => DEFAULT_TERMINAL_ID)
)
```

### DEPENDENCY RELATIONSHIPS

```
apps/web
  ├── depends on: @t3tools/contracts (types for state, WebSocket)
  └── depends on: @t3tools/shared/model (model normalization)

apps/server
  ├── depends on: @t3tools/contracts (types for orchestration, persistence)
  ├── depends on: @t3tools/shared/Net (port discovery)
  └── depends on: @t3tools/shared/logging (file logging)

@t3tools/shared
  └── depends on: @t3tools/contracts (uses model types from contracts)

@t3tools/contracts
  └── depends on: effect (Schema library only)
```

### KEY OBSERVATIONS

1. **Clear separation of concerns**: `contracts` = types/schemas, `shared` = implementations
2. **Effect-based architecture**: Heavy use of Effect's Schema for validation and branded types
3. **No bidirectional dependencies**: contracts is foundational, shared imports from contracts
4. **Direct source consumption**: Both packages served as ESM source files to dev apps
5. **Branded IDs everywhere**: All entity IDs are branded strings for type safety
6. **Comprehensive protocol definitions**: WebSocket and IPC protocols fully typed via schemas
7. **65+ Schema types** across contracts covering all domain entities
8. **Minimal external dependencies**: Only `effect` as runtime dependency (and platform-specific bindings)

I'm realizing the approach is getting complicated with conditional logic for different themes, so I'm simplifying to just apply all colors as inline styles consistently across every theme instead. I see the issue now—I need to override the existing CSS variables like `--background` and `--foreground` directly rather than creating new ones, since the existing styles already reference those. For the additional theme colors like sidebar and editor backgrounds, I can introduce new variables. This way, the theme colors will actually apply to the UI instead of being ignored. I'm realizing that setting all CSS variables via JavaScript would actually break the existing cascade—computed values like `--card` that reference `--background` would use the CSS-defined value, not the inline style override. But wait, CSS custom properties do cascade normally, so if I set `--background` inline on the root element, the `var(--background)` reference inside the `--card` definition should pick up that inline value instead.

Let me verify: when `--card` is defined in the stylesheet as a `color-mix` that references `var(--background)`, and I set `--background` via inline style on the same element, the computed `--card` value will use the inline `--background` because inline styles have the highest priority. So I only need to set the base variables like `--background` and `--foreground` via JavaScript, and the dependent variables will cascade correctly.

For the three themes, I can keep it simple: `t3-light` and `t3-dark` just toggle the `.dark` class and set the new component-specific variables, while `t3-midnight` adds darker overrides for those same base variables.

The tricky part is handling the cleanup when switching themes—if I always set all the override variables explicitly, even when they match the CSS defaults, I avoid the complexity of removing properties. So `t3-light` would set `--background` to white (matching the default), `t3-dark` would set it to the dark color, and `t3-midnight` would set it to something even darker. The component will need to import the color definitions from the shared theme package so it can render accurate previews without actually applying the theme. I'm also checking whether the shared package's tsconfig has composite mode enabled...

Checking the tsconfig configuration...

Looking at the shared package's exports, I can add a new `./theme` export pointing to the source file without needing any tsconfig changes since the src directory is already included. Now I need to verify the web app's tsconfig will properly resolve this new import path.

Since the web app already imports from `@t3tools/shared` and has it as a dependency, the new theme export should resolve the same way through the workspace configuration. I'm ready to write out the implementation plan.

Defining the theme interface... Defining the theme objects...

Now I need to update the package exports and rewrite the useTheme hook to handle the new theme system, using a storage key of `"t3code:theme"` and migrating from the old light/dark/system values to the new theme IDs.

Then I should figure out where the `ThemeProvider` fits in the architecture—it probably belongs in the hooks file as a named export that wraps the app and applies the theme from the store. keeps things consolidated. Let me verify that `DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` still works with the updated hook—it only destructures `resolvedTheme`, so even though the return type now includes `themeId` and `theme`, the existing code remains compatible. Now I need to check if `_chat.settings.tsx` requires the `theme` and `setTheme` exports from the old API.

Looking at the settings view, it currently destructures `theme`, `setTheme`, and `resolvedTheme` to power the radio group for theme selection and display the active theme. With the new hook, `theme` becomes `themeId`, `setTheme` becomes `setThemeId`, and `resolvedTheme` stays the same. Since we're replacing the entire theme selection UI with a `<ThemeSelector />` component, I'll need to remove the old theme-related JSX from settings and swap it in.

For the display text, instead of showing "light" or "dark" from `resolvedTheme`, I can show the theme's display name using `theme.name` for something more user-friendly.

Now I'm verifying the provider setup—the `ThemeProvider` should wrap the app in the router configuration, nesting inside the existing `QueryClientProvider` and `StoreProvider`. This placement works fine since the provider doesn't need any special context, just needs to be part of React's rendering tree.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a custom theme system to the T3 Code editor. Here is the full context:

## Codebase Summary

**Monorepo**: Bun workspaces, Turbo, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Packages:
- `packages/shared` — shared runtime utilities; uses subpath exports (`./model`, `./git`, etc.) pointing directly to TypeScript source; no build step needed; has `effect` dependency
- `packages/contracts` — Schema-based type contracts; barrel export; compiled
- `apps/web` — React 19 + Vite 8 + Tailwind CSS 4 + TanStack Router + Zustand
- `apps/server` — Node.js WebSocket server

**Constraint**: No build config changes, no new dependencies.

## Existing Theme System (to be replaced)

`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`:
- Stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in `localStorage["t3code:theme"]`
- Applies `.dark` CSS class on `<html>` for Tailwind dark mode
- Uses `useSyncExternalStore` module-level external store pattern (NO React Context)
- Module-level `applyTheme(getStored())` call fires immediately to prevent flash
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` where `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"`

Current consumers of `useTheme`:
1. `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` — uses `theme`, `setTheme`, `resolvedTheme`
2. `apps/web/src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` — uses only `resolvedTheme`

**CSS** (`apps/web/src/index.css`):
- Tailwind CSS 4 with `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))`
- CSS custom properties in `:root` for light, and `@variant dark { }` inside `:root` for dark
- Vars include: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--accent`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.
- No `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg` vars exist yet
- Has `.no-transitions` class for suppressing transitions during theme changes

**Provider composition** (`apps/web/src/router.ts`):
```ts
Wrap: ({ children }) =>
  createElement(QueryClientProvider, { client: queryClient },
    createElement(StoreProvider, null, children))
```

**Settings page** (`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`):
- Has "Appearance" section with radio buttons for `THEME_OPTIONS` (system/light/dark)
- Imports and uses `useTheme`

**Shared package subpath exports** (`packages/shared/package.json`):
```json
"exports": {
  "./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" },
  "./git": { "types": "./src/git.ts", "import": "./src/git.ts" },
  // ... etc
}
```
tsconfig for shared: `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "include": ["src"] }` — no `composite: true`.

## Task Requirements

1. **Define a theme type in the shared package** with at least 3 built-in themes: "t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight" — covering colors for: background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, status bar

2. **Create a theme provider and useTheme hook in the web app** that persists selection to localStorage and applies CSS custom properties

3. **Add a theme selector component with preview swatches**

## Key Design Decisions to Include in Plan

**A. Theme type location**: Add `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (new file) with `./theme` subpath export added to `packages/shared/package.json`. Use plain TypeScript (no Effect Schema) since the server doesn't consume this. The `apps/web` already depends on `@t3tools/shared`.

**B. CSS custom property application strategy**: When a theme is applied, use `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` to inject inline styles (highest CSS specificity, overrides all stylesheets). This is the single-source-of-truth approach. Theme colors are defined in `theme.ts` and applied via JS. For `t3-light` and `t3-dark`, we still toggle `.dark` for Tailwind compat. The `color-mix()` / `--alpha()` vars in index.css cascade correctly since `--background` inline style takes precedence, and other vars like `--card: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, ...)` pick up the new `--background` correctly.

**C. CSS custom properties to set**: 
- Override existing: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` (Tailwind + UI components use these)
- Add new: `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg` (for the theme-specific areas and swatches)

**D. No "system" option**: The new system drops the "system" option. Migration: old "system" or "dark" stored values → "t3-dark"; old "light" → "t3-light"; invalid → DEFAULT_THEME_ID ("t3-dark").

**E. Hook API**: `useTheme()` returns `{ themeId: ThemeId, theme: AppTheme, setThemeId: (id: ThemeId) => void, resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark" }`. The `resolvedTheme` is `theme.dark ? "dark" : "light"`. DiffWorkerPoolProvider still works unchanged.

**F. ThemeProvider component**: Export `ThemeProvider` from `useTheme.ts` — a thin component that calls `useTheme()` internally to ensure DOM sync effects run. Added to `router.ts` Wrap chain. This satisfies the "theme provider" requirement without React Context overhead.

**G. ThemeSelector component**: New `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`. Shows 3 cards side-by-side with a mini UI mockup preview (sidebar strip + editor area + terminal area + status bar strip) using inline background colors from `BUILT_IN_THEMES[id].colors`. Clicking selects the theme. Active theme shows highlighted border and "Selected" badge. Used in `_chat.settings.tsx` to replace the existing radio buttons.

**H. Concrete color values**: Use hex or oklch values consistent with the existing palette:
- t3-light: `background: "#ffffff"`, `foreground: "#262626"`, `accent: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)"` (matches existing light vars), `sidebar: "#f9fafb"`, `editor: "#ffffff"`, `terminal: "#1c1c1c"`, `statusBar: "#1f2937"`
- t3-dark: `background: "#0f0f10"`, `foreground: "#f5f5f5"`, `accent: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)"` (matches existing dark vars), `sidebar: "#111113"`, `editor: "#0a0a0b"`, `terminal: "#0a0a0b"`, `statusBar: "#1e1e22"`  
- t3-midnight: `background: "#000000"`, `foreground: "#e5e5e5"`, `accent: "#a78bfa"` (violet — visually distinct), `sidebar: "#050507"`, `editor: "#000000"`, `terminal: "#020204"`, `statusBar: "#0f0f12"`

Please design a complete step-by-step implementation plan including:
- All files to create and modify (with exact file paths)
- The precise code changes needed for each file
- Any edge cases (migration from old theme storage, clearing old inline styles when changing themes)
- Verification steps

Return a detailed plan.

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a custom theme system to the T3 Code editor. Here is the full context:

## Codebase Summary

**Monorepo**: Bun workspaces, Turbo, TypeScript strict mode throughout. Packages:
- `packages/shared` — shared runtime utilities; uses subpath exports (`./model`, `./git`, etc.) pointing directly to TypeScript source; no build step needed; has `effect` dependency
- `packages/contracts` — Schema-based type contracts; barrel export; compiled
- `apps/web` — React 19 + Vite 8 + Tailwind CSS 4 + TanStack Router + Zustand
- `apps/server` — Node.js WebSocket server

**Constraint**: No build config changes, no new dependencies.

## Existing Theme System (to be replaced)

`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`:
- Stores `"light" | "dark" | "system"` in `localStorage["t3code:theme"]`
- Applies `.dark` CSS class on `<html>` for Tailwind dark mode
- Uses `useSyncExternalStore` module-level external store pattern (NO React Context)
- Module-level `applyTheme(getStored())` call fires immediately to prevent flash
- Returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` where `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"`

Current consumers of `useTheme`:
1. `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` — uses `theme`, `setTheme`, `resolvedTheme`
2. `apps/web/src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` — uses only `resolvedTheme`

**CSS** (`apps/web/src/index.css`):
- Tailwind CSS 4 with `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))`
- CSS custom properties in `:root` for light, and `@variant dark { }` inside `:root` for dark
- Vars include: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--accent`, `--muted`, `--border`, etc.
- No `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg` vars exist yet
- Has `.no-transitions` class for suppressing transitions during theme changes

**Provider composition** (`apps/web/src/router.ts`):
```ts
Wrap: ({ children }) =>
  createElement(QueryClientProvider, { client: queryClient },
    createElement(StoreProvider, null, children))
```

**Settings page** (`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`):
- Has "Appearance" section with radio buttons for `THEME_OPTIONS` (system/light/dark)
- Imports and uses `useTheme`

**Shared package subpath exports** (`packages/shared/package.json`):
```json
"exports": {
  "./model": { "types": "./src/model.ts", "import": "./src/model.ts" },
  "./git": { "types": "./src/git.ts", "import": "./src/git.ts" },
  // ... etc
}
```
tsconfig for shared: `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "include": ["src"] }` — no `composite: true`.

## Task Requirements

1. **Define a theme type in the shared package** with at least 3 built-in themes: "t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight" — covering colors for: background, foreground, accent, sidebar, editor, terminal, status bar

2. **Create a theme provider and useTheme hook in the web app** that persists selection to localStorage and applies CSS custom properties

3. **Add a theme selector component with preview swatches**

## Key Design Decisions to Include in Plan

**A. Theme type location**: Add `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (new file) with `./theme` subpath export added to `packages/shared/package.json`. Use plain TypeScript (no Effect Schema) since the server doesn't consume this. The `apps/web` already depends on `@t3tools/shared`.

**B. CSS custom property application strategy**: When a theme is applied, use `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` to inject inline styles (highest CSS specificity, overrides all stylesheets). This is the single-source-of-truth approach. Theme colors are defined in `theme.ts` and applied via JS. For `t3-light` and `t3-dark`, we still toggle `.dark` for Tailwind compat. The `color-mix()` / `--alpha()` vars in index.css cascade correctly since `--background` inline style takes precedence, and other vars like `--card: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 98%, ...)` pick up the new `--background` correctly.

**C. CSS custom properties to set**: 
- Override existing: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring` (Tailwind + UI components use these)
- Add new: `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg` (for the theme-specific areas and swatches)

**D. No "system" option**: The new system drops the "system" option. Migration: old "system" or "dark" stored values → "t3-dark"; old "light" → "t3-light"; invalid → DEFAULT_THEME_ID ("t3-dark").

**E. Hook API**: `useTheme()` returns `{ themeId: ThemeId, theme: AppTheme, setThemeId: (id: ThemeId) => void, resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark" }`. The `resolvedTheme` is `theme.dark ? "dark" : "light"`. DiffWorkerPoolProvider still works unchanged.

**F. ThemeProvider component**: Export `ThemeProvider` from `useTheme.ts` — a thin component that calls `useTheme()` internally to ensure DOM sync effects run. Added to `router.ts` Wrap chain. This satisfies the "theme provider" requirement without React Context overhead.

**G. ThemeSelector component**: New `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`. Shows 3 cards side-by-side with a mini UI mockup preview (sidebar strip + editor area + terminal area + status bar strip) using inline background colors from `BUILT_IN_THEMES[id].colors`. Clicking selects the theme. Active theme shows highlighted border and "Selected" badge. Used in `_chat.settings.tsx` to replace the existing radio buttons.

**H. Concrete color values**: Use hex or oklch values consistent with the existing palette:
- t3-light: `background: "#ffffff"`, `foreground: "#262626"`, `accent: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)"` (matches existing light vars), `sidebar: "#f9fafb"`, `editor: "#ffffff"`, `terminal: "#1c1c1c"`, `statusBar: "#1f2937"`
- t3-dark: `background: "#0f0f10"`, `foreground: "#f5f5f5"`, `accent: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)"` (matches existing dark vars), `sidebar: "#111113"`, `editor: "#0a0a0b"`, `terminal: "#0a0a0b"`, `statusBar: "#1e1e22"`  
- t3-midnight: `background: "#000000"`, `foreground: "#e5e5e5"`, `accent: "#a78bfa"` (violet — visually distinct), `sidebar: "#050507"`, `editor: "#000000"`, `terminal: "#020204"`, `statusBar: "#0f0f12"`

Please design a complete step-by-step implementation plan including:
- All files to create and modify (with exact file paths)
- The precise code changes needed for each file
- Any edge cases (migration from old theme storage, clearing old inline styles when changing themes)
- Verification steps

Return a detailed plan.

input.summary:
## Theme System Implementation Plan

Here's what I'll build across **6 files** (2 new, 4 edited):

---

### New files
1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — `ThemeColors`, `AppTheme`, `ThemeId`, `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `isThemeId`, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID`. Pure TypeScript, no Effect dependencies. Three themes:
   - `t3-dark` — dark blue-black, blue accent
   - `t3-light` — white, blue accent  
   - `t3-midnight` — true black, violet accent (visually distinct from t3-dark)

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — 3-column card grid. Each card renders a mini UI mockup (sidebar strip + editor + terminal + status bar) using **inline colors from the theme's `colors` object**, so all 3 cards always show their own palette regardless of the active theme. Active card gets a `border-primary` outline and "Active" badge.

---

### Edited files

3. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — add `"./theme"` subpath export (same pattern as existing `./model`, `./git`, etc.)

4. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — full replacement. Key changes:
   - Stores a `ThemeId` instead of `"light"|"dark"|"system"`
   - `applyTheme()` calls `document.documentElement.style.setProperty()` for each color (inline styles override all CSS, and cascade correctly into derived vars like `--card: color-mix(var(--background)…)`)
   - Migration handles old stored values: `"dark"/"system"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`
   - Exports `ThemeProvider` — thin component that calls `useTheme()` to ensure DOM sync effects run at the root
   - `resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark"` preserved for `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` (no changes needed there)

5. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — remove `THEME_OPTIONS` + radio buttons; replace with `<ThemeSelector />`; update `useTheme` destructuring

6. **`apps/web/src/router.ts`** — wrap the `Wrap` chain with `ThemeProvider` as the outermost element

---

### What stays unchanged
- `index.css` — existing `:root` light defaults and `@variant dark` become fallback-only; inline styles override them
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx` — still reads `resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark"`, no API change
- All build config, all dependencies

Here's the complete implementation plan:

---

## Theme System Plan — 6 Files, 0 New Dependencies

### What I explored
- The existing `useTheme` hook uses a `useSyncExternalStore` module-level store, stores `"light"|"dark"|"system"`, and applies just a `.dark` CSS class
- The settings page has radio buttons for the 3 mode options
- `@t3tools/shared` uses subpath exports pointing directly to TS source — adding `./theme` is a one-liner
- `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` only reads `resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark"` — needs no changes
- `index.css` defines CSS custom properties in `:root` — inline styles via `style.setProperty()` take precedence and cascade correctly into derived vars like `--card: color-mix(var(--background)…)`

### Plan summary

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | **New** — `ThemeColors`, `AppTheme`, `ThemeId`, `BUILT_IN_THEMES`, `isThemeId` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | **Edit** — add `"./theme"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **Replace** — new hook, `applyTheme` via `style.setProperty()`, storage migration, `ThemeProvider` |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **New** — 3-card grid with mini UI mockup previews |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **Edit** — swap radio buttons for `<ThemeSelector />` |
| `apps/web/src/router.ts` | **Edit** — add `ThemeProvider` to `Wrap` chain |

### Key design decisions
- **Inline styles** (`style.setProperty`) for CSS custom properties — highest specificity, single source of truth in `theme.ts`, and correctly cascades into derived vars
- **`t3-midnight`** uses violet accent (`#a78bfa`) to be visually distinct from `t3-dark`'s blue
- **Migration**: old `"dark"/"system"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`, non-destructive
- **`ThemeProvider`** is a thin wrapper (no React Context) — it calls `useTheme()` to ensure the DOM-sync `useEffect` fires at the root level
- **Preview cards** use inline `backgroundColor` directly from `theme.colors` so all 3 always show their own palette regardless of which theme is active

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was implemented

### `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` *(new)*
Defines the theme type contract: `ThemeColors` (7 fields: background, foreground, accent, sidebarBg, editorBg, terminalBg, statusBarBg), `AppTheme` (id, label, dark flag, colors), `ThemeId` union, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` record with all three built-ins, `THEME_IDS` array, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID`, and a `isThemeId()` type guard. Pure TypeScript — no Effect dependency.

### `packages/shared/package.json` *(edited)*
Added `"./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" }` to `exports`, following the identical pattern of all other subpath exports.

### `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` *(replaced)*
- Stores a `ThemeId` (e.g. `"t3-midnight"`) instead of `"light"|"dark"|"system"` in `localStorage["t3code:theme"]`
- `migrateStoredValue()` handles old stored values: `"dark"/"system"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`
- `applyTheme()` injects inline `style.setProperty()` calls for all 8 CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring`, `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg`) plus `color-scheme`, then toggles `.dark` for Tailwind compat
- `useTheme()` returns `{ themeId, theme, setThemeId, resolvedTheme }` — `resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark"` is unchanged so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` needs no edits
- Exports `ThemeProvider` — a thin wrapper that calls `useTheme()` to ensure DOM-sync effects run at the root

### `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` *(new)*
3-column card grid. Each card shows a mini UI mockup (sidebar strip + editor + terminal + status bar) using **inline colors from the theme object** — all three cards always display their own palette regardless of which theme is active. The active card gets a `border-primary` outline and an "Active" badge.

### `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` *(edited)*
Removed the `THEME_OPTIONS` const and the radio-button loop; replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`. Updated `useTheme` destructuring to only pull `resolvedTheme`.

### `apps/web/src/router.ts` *(edited)*
Added `ThemeProvider` as the outermost wrapper in the `Wrap` chain, so the theme DOM-sync effect fires once for the entire app lifetime before any route renders.

**`bun lint`**: 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings in untouched server files)  
**`bun typecheck`**: errors in marketing/contracts/shared are all pre-existing environment issues (missing `astro`, `effect`, `vite` type roots) — none introduced by this change.

## What was implemented

### `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` *(new)*
Defines the theme type contract: `ThemeColors` (7 fields: background, foreground, accent, sidebarBg, editorBg, terminalBg, statusBarBg), `AppTheme` (id, label, dark flag, colors), `ThemeId` union, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` record with all three built-ins, `THEME_IDS` array, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID`, and a `isThemeId()` type guard. Pure TypeScript — no Effect dependency.

### `packages/shared/package.json` *(edited)*
Added `"./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" }` to `exports`, following the identical pattern of all other subpath exports.

### `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` *(replaced)*
- Stores a `ThemeId` (e.g. `"t3-midnight"`) instead of `"light"|"dark"|"system"` in `localStorage["t3code:theme"]`
- `migrateStoredValue()` handles old stored values: `"dark"/"system"` → `"t3-dark"`, `"light"` → `"t3-light"`
- `applyTheme()` injects inline `style.setProperty()` calls for all 8 CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--ring`, `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg`) plus `color-scheme`, then toggles `.dark` for Tailwind compat
- `useTheme()` returns `{ themeId, theme, setThemeId, resolvedTheme }` — `resolvedTheme: "light"|"dark"` is unchanged so `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` needs no edits
- Exports `ThemeProvider` — a thin wrapper that calls `useTheme()` to ensure DOM-sync effects run at the root

### `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` *(new)*
3-column card grid. Each card shows a mini UI mockup (sidebar strip + editor + terminal + status bar) using **inline colors from the theme object** — all three cards always display their own palette regardless of which theme is active. The active card gets a `border-primary` outline and an "Active" badge.

### `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` *(edited)*
Removed the `THEME_OPTIONS` const and the radio-button loop; replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`. Updated `useTheme` destructuring to only pull `resolvedTheme`.

### `apps/web/src/router.ts` *(edited)*
Added `ThemeProvider` as the outermost wrapper in the `Wrap` chain, so the theme DOM-sync effect fires once for the entire app lifetime before any route renders.

**`bun lint`**: 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings in untouched server files)  
**`bun typecheck`**: errors in marketing/contracts/shared are all pre-existing environment issues (missing `astro`, `effect`, `vite` type roots) — none introduced by this change.
